description: Coverage targets for Jetpack across REST API, database schema, server requests, and browser requests.

options.coverage_goal: bounded Jetpack option read/write/default shape coverage for isolated fixture options, including serialized values and rollback verification

modules.coverage_goal: bounded Jetpack module activate/deactivate/settings state matrix plus module option/table inventory in an isolated runner

sync.coverage_goal: bounded Jetpack sync queue/action serialization, cron event inventory, and queue option delta coverage without WordPress.com credentials

connection_fixtures.coverage_goal: connected/disconnected site fixture boundaries, token placeholder serialization, and WP.com-dependent skip classifications

public_frontend.coverage_goal: public Jetpack module frontend rendering and request scenarios with connected/disconnected expectations

performance_observation.coverage_goal: Jetpack admin, REST, sync, module, frontend, query-count, request-timing, and external HTTP guardrail summaries

jetpack-rest-route-inventory.coverage_shape: registered Jetpack and WPCOM REST route inventory with permission-boundary classification

notes: Inspects the local REST route registry
Does not execute route handlers

generated-rest-request-cases.coverage_shape: generated safe Jetpack REST request cases for site, connection, modules, settings, and WPCOM publicize routes

notes: Uses explicit local GET request cases
Does not require WordPress.com OAuth credentials

rest-db-query-profile.coverage_shape: DB query profile for safe Jetpack REST request cases

notes: Profiles local REST request cases only
Captures query counts without external service calls

db-inventory.coverage_shape: Jetpack fixture database table, option, module-state, row, column, and index inventory

notes: Reads isolated runner database metadata
Inventories Jetpack table prefixes and module/options state
Does not mutate production or developer databases

jetpack-admin-page-coverage.coverage_shape: bounded authenticated wp-admin Jetpack menu and submenu GET coverage for administrator users

notes: GET-only admin visits with explicit skips for destructive/update/install screens
Per-role and per-page limits bound request volume

jetpack-module-state-matrix.coverage_shape: bounded Jetpack module activation, deactivation, dependency, and settings-state matrix

notes: Mutates only module state in the isolated runner
Connection-dependent modules are classified instead of calling external services

jetpack-module-option-table-inventory.coverage_shape: read-only Jetpack module option, autoload, serialized-value, and custom-table inventory for module-state fuzz inputs

notes: Reads isolated runner options and table metadata only
Secret-like option values are represented by placeholder classifications instead of raw values

jetpack-options-matrix.coverage_shape: bounded Jetpack option defaults, serialization, update, and rollback matrix

notes: Mutates options only inside the isolated runner database
Secrets and tokens are represented by synthetic placeholder values
Every mutation must record before/after/restore rows so failed assertions can roll back safely

jetpack-sync-queue-coverage.coverage_shape: bounded Jetpack sync queue, action serialization, cron, and retry coverage without remote dispatch

notes: Exercises local sync queue state only
Outbound WordPress.com dispatch is blocked by the external HTTP guardrail
Queue option deltas must be restored before the run exits

jetpack-cron-sync-actions.coverage_shape: Jetpack cron event, local sync action, queue option delta, and rollback-safe cron mutation inventory without remote dispatch

notes: Schedules and clears only synthetic cron events in the isolated runner
Blocks outbound dispatch with the Jetpack external HTTP guardrail
Records rollback rows for cron and queue option mutations

jetpack-connected-disconnected-fixtures.coverage_shape: connected and disconnected Jetpack fixture-state matrix for REST, module, sync, token-placeholder serialization, and WP.com-dependent skip classifications

notes: Uses synthetic placeholder connection values only
Records connected_required, disconnected_expected, and credential_unavailable skip reasons instead of contacting WordPress.com

jetpack-public-module-frontend-coverage.coverage_shape: public-module frontend rendering and request coverage for representative Jetpack modules across connected and disconnected fixture states

notes: Runs against local fixture pages and posts in WP Codebox
External module calls are blocked or classified by the Jetpack HTTP guardrail

jetpack-performance-observation.coverage_shape: Jetpack admin, settings, REST, sync queue, module frontend, request timing, query count, asset, and external HTTP guardrail summary contract

notes: Summarizes executable coverage expectations without claiming proof until run artifacts exist
Heavy proof runs should use offloaded Homeboy runners instead of local benchmarks

jetpack-external-http-guardrail.coverage_shape: Jetpack connection, sync, module, and WordPress.com API external HTTP guardrail probes with blocked synthetic requests and declared WP.com allowlist boundaries

notes: Installs the external HTTP guardrail before probes run
Uses .invalid synthetic hosts for blocked probes
Declares public-api.wordpress.com as an allowlisted boundary without making live service calls

jetpack-browser-coverage.coverage_shape: Jetpack dashboard, connection, modules, and settings admin browser request coverage

notes: Runs in WP Codebox browser coverage
No WordPress.com OAuth fixture or live service account is provisioned
